company.title: Who We Are
company.paragraph1: Sky High Financial Management Inc. is committed to delivering accurate accounting, tax, and financial solutions tailored to the unique needs of each client.
company.paragraph2: We believe accounting should provide clarity—not complexity. Our goal is to help business owners understand their financial position, remain compliant, reduce tax risk, and make confident decisions for future growth.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.text: To provide reliable, professional accounting, bookkeeping, payroll, and tax services that help individuals and businesses make informed financial decisions.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.text: To become one of Canada's most trusted accounting firms by delivering exceptional service, modern technology, and long-term client relationships.

values.title: Our Values
values.text: Integrity, professionalism, transparency, responsiveness, and genuine commitment to our clients' success.

faq.title: Questions We Often Receive
faq.description: Here are answers to some of the most common questions about our accounting and tax services.

q1.question: Do you work with clients remotely?
q1.answer: Yes. We work with individuals and businesses across Canada using secure online communication and cloud-based accounting tools.

q2.question: Do you provide services outside British Columbia?
q2.answer: Absolutely. We proudly serve clients across all Canadian provinces and territories.

q3.question: How quickly will I receive a response?
q3.answer: We aim to respond to all inquiries within one business day.

q4.question: Can I schedule an online consultation?
q4.answer: Yes. We offer virtual consultations for both personal and business clients anywhere in Canada.

deadlines.heading: CRA Tax Deadlines
deadlines.description: Below are some of the most common Canadian tax filing and payment deadlines for individuals and businesses. Your specific deadlines may vary depending on your filing status and reporting requirements.

personal.description: Personal income tax filing and payment deadline (most individuals).

selfEmployed.description: Self-employed personal income tax filing deadline. Any balance owing is generally due April 30.

corporatePayment.description: Corporate income tax payment deadline (depending on CRA eligibility rules).

t2.description: T2 Corporation Income Tax Return filing deadline.

gst.description: GST/HST return and payment deadline (depends on your filing frequency).

payroll.description: Payroll remittance deadlines depend on your CRA remitter type and reporting frequency.

note.title: Important Note
note.description: Filing and payment deadlines may vary depending on your business structure, fiscal year-end, reporting frequency, and eligibility under CRA rules. For example, some Canadian-controlled private corporations (CCPCs) qualify for a three-month corporate tax payment deadline, while others must pay within two months. Always refer to the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) for the most current requirements.
